channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Koreans training in Russia are now in combat zone: Ukraine Defense Intelligence
item.description: North Korean forces have entered the combat zone between Russian and Ukrainian forces for the first time after training at Kremlin military bases, Ukraine’s defense intelligence service said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: A car bomb explodes outside a police station in western Mexico, wounding 3 officers
item.description: ACAMBARO, Mexico (AP) — A car bomb left outside a police station in the town of Acambaro in western Mexico wounded three people, prosecutors in the violence-wracked state of Guanajuato said Thursday. They said another explosion, apparently a second car bomb, occurred in the nearby town of Jerecuaro.
item.dc:creator: ARMANDO SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US on alert for election interference from Russia, China, Iran
item.description: Microsoft said in a report Wednesday that foreign adversaries have shown continued determination to influence the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Navalny widow to Americans: Don't take democracy for granted
item.description: Yulia Navalnaya, the widow of the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny, encouraged Americans not to take democracy for granted, in an interview on ABC’s “The View” that aired Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Argentine police raid the Buenos Aires hotel where One Direction’s Liam Payne died
item.description: The police have raided the Buenos Aires hotel where ex-One Direction singer Liam Payne stayed before dying last week after falling from a third-floor balcony.
item.dc:creator: Almudena Calatrava, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza shelter kills 17 as Blinken says cease-fire talks will resume
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a school where displaced people were sheltering in the central Gaza Strip killed at least 17 people on Thursday, nearly all women and children, Palestinian medical officials said. The strike came as U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, FARNOUSH AMIRI and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken warns Israel 'should not lead a protracted campaign' against Hezbollah in Lebanon
item.description: Secretary of State Blinken says, nearly a month into the Israeli military's ground operations in Lebanon, that it should not lead to a 'protracted campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia spread false claims about Helene, Milton, government response: Report
item.description: Russia spread false claims about the U.S. government’s response to hurricanes Helene and Milton in an effort to sow discontent and undermine public support for Ukraine, according to a new report from the Institute for Strategic Dialogue (ISD).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Canada's Trudeau vows lead his Liberal Party into the next election
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday that he will lead his Liberal Party into the next election, dismissing a request by some party members to not run for a fourth term.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Train carrying 55 people derails on Norway's north coast, killing at least 1 person and injuring 4
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — At least one person was killed and four people injured when a train running along Norway’s northern coast derailed Thursday with 55 people on board, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trash carried by a North Korean balloon again falls on the presidential compound in Seoul
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Trash carried by a North Korean balloon fell on the presidential compound in central Seoul on Thursday for the second time, raising concerns about the vulnerability of key South Korean sites.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mozambique's ruling party candidate declared winner of presidential election as rigging claims swirl
item.description: Moazmbique’s ruling party candidate Daniel Chapo has been declared the winner of the country’s presidential election amid claims of rigging by the opposition.
item.dc:creator: Charles Mangwiro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US announces $135M in humanitarian assistance to Palestinians
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken on Thursday announced $135 million in new humanitarian assistance for Palestinians and said American negotiators will meet with Israeli and Qatari counterparts to revive talks on a cease-fire despite uncertainty over Hamas’s participation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Tropical storm battering Philippines leaves at least 24 people dead in flooding and landslides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Widespread flooding and landslides set off by a tropical storm in the northeastern Philippines on Thursday left at least 24 people dead, swept away cars and prompted authorities to scramble for motorboats to rescue trapped villagers, some on roofs.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia says its coast guard drove away Chinese ship that interrupted survey in disputed sea
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian patrol ships drove a Chinese coast guard vessel away from a survey vessel in a disputed area of the South China Sea for the second time in three days, Indonesian authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin ends BRICS summit that sought to expand Russia's global clout but was shadowed by Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has presided at the closing session of a summit of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, hailing its role as a counterbalance to the West.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian lawmakers ratify pact with North Korea as US confirms that Pyongyang sent troops to Russia
item.description: Russian lawmakers have ratified a pact with North Korea envisioning mutual military assistance, a move that comes even as the U.S. confirmed the deployment of 3,000 North Korean troops to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Climate-threatened Pacific Islands criticize Australia at Commonwealth meeting
item.description: APIA, Samoa (AP) — Several Pacific island nations singled out Australia to do more to phase out fossil fuel exports during the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting which opened in Samoa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man pleads guilty to displaying a Nazi symbol at pro-Palestinian rally
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — A restaurateur pleaded guilty Thursday to displaying a Nazi symbol during a pro-Palestinian rally in Sydney in early October. Alan Yazbek was photographed at the Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive displacement from Israel-Hezbollah war transforms Beirut’s famed commercial street
item.description: Inside what was once one of Beirut’s oldest and best-known cinemas, dozens of Lebanese, Palestinians and Syrians displaced by the Israel-Hezbollah war now spend their time following the news on their phones, cooking, chatting or walking around to pass the time.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel accuses 6 Al Jazeera journalists in Gaza of being Palestinian militants
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The Israeli army on Wednesday accused six Al Jazeera journalists covering the war in Gaza of also being current or former paid fighters for Palestinian militant groups. Al Jazeera rejected the claims.
item.dc:creator: JACK JEFFERY and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria following deadly attack on defense company
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey’s air force struck Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria on Wednesday in an apparent retaliation for an attack at a key state-run defense company that killed five people and wounded more than 20 others.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Liberal lawmakers ask Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau not to run for a fourth term
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Some lawmakers in Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's own Liberal Party asked the leader not to run for a fourth term Wednesday, handing him one of the biggest tests of his political career.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli weapon seen in rare AP photos of Beirut airstrike appears to be a powerful smart bomb
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — In all but the blink of an eye, an Associated Press photographer's camera captured the moments that a battleship-gray Israeli bomb plummeted toward a Beirut building before detonating to bring the tower down.
item.dc:creator: ADAM SCHRECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Situation in Haiti worsens amid ‘acute violence’ as UN support mission falters under gang violence
item.description: The gang violence in Haiti has gotten worse despite the deployment of U.N.-sanctioned international police officers, forcing mass displacement, hunger among millions and even famine for thousands of Haitians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: US scrambles after Israel war plan leak: What to know
item.description: An apparent U.S. intelligence breach related to Israel's plans to strike Iran has unsettled the intelligence community and sent the Biden administration scrambling to determine if it came from a hack or an internal leak.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Woman wedged between boulders in Australia for hours
item.description: The woman had apparently dropped her phone and was trying to retrieve it while hiking in Australia, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine blasts UN chief over potential meeting with Putin at BRICS, says stayed away from Kyiv's peace summit
item.description: Ukraine blasted U.N. Secretary-General Guterres for allegedly agreeing to meet with Russian President Putin during the BRICS summit as leaders from the West's top adversaries like China and Iran convene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy says a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway Germany
item.description: Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his “victory plan” after the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing student in Spain was likely crushed, incinerated after passing out in trash while partying: cops
item.description: A student missing in Majorca, Spain, was likely crushed to death in a garbage truck before she was incinerated at a waste disposal plant, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gunmen kill 7 people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Gunmen fatally shot at least seven people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir and injured at least five others, officials said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Italian surfer dies in freak accident after swordfish impales her while surfing in Indonesia
item.description: An Italian surfer hunting for waves off the west coast of Sumatra died after she was stabbed in the chest by the bill of a swordfish, in what is being considered a freak accident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation
